stub: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her decisions help to promote the national interest.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said:  “Elections are important because they force me as a politician to listen and respond to the views of voters.”
text: She is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with her own beliefs, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly explains how her decisions help to promote the national interest. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said:  “Elections are important because they force me as a politician to listen and respond to the views of voters.”
